name: Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas
name_original: Embalse de Colomera — circular por Tózar y Benalúa de las Villas
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Colomera traverse a diverse landscape characterized by varied terrain, from gentle ascents to steeper climbs. Situated at an altitude of 862 meters, the region features scrubland, olive groves, and wooded areas. The Colomera Reservoir is a prominent natural feature, with many routes offering views of its waters. Cyclists can expect a mix of challenging and rewarding conditions across this topography. Best touring cycling routes around Colomera The most popular touring cycling route is Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas, a 20.9 miles (33.7 km) trail that takes 2 hours 32 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain and views of the Colomera Reservoir. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Gollizno Route and Moclín Castle Loop, a difficult 14.7 miles (23.6 km) path. This route offers a mix of climbs and descents through varied terrain, including historical points of interest. Local touring cyclists also love the Cubillas Reservoir – Roman Dam of Barcinas loop from Embalse de Cubillas, a 19.9 miles (32.0 km) trail leading through olive groves and past the Cubillas Reservoir, often completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes. Touring cycling around Colomera is defined by varied terrain, olive groves, and the Colomera Reservoir. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging rides. The routes in Colomera are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 10 reviews. More than 200 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Colomera's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Colomera?
A: There are over 25 touring cycling routes around Colomera, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists. The komoot community has explored these routes over 200 times, highlighting the region's appeal.

Q: What is the typical difficulty level of touring cycling routes in Colomera?
A: Colomera offers a mix of cycling challenges. While there are 8 moderate routes, the majority, 21 routes, are classified as difficult. Cyclists can expect varied terrain with both gentle ascents and steeper climbs, making it a rewarding destination for those seeking a challenge.

Q: What kind of landscapes and terrain can I expect while touring cycling in Colomera?
A: The region around Colomera, situated at 862 meters above sea level, features diverse topography. You'll cycle through scrubland, olive groves, and wooded areas, with routes offering a mix of climbs and descents. Many paths also provide picturesque views of the Colomera Reservoir and pass through aromatic plant fields with rosemary, thyme, and lavender.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in Colomera?
A: Yes, many routes in Colomera are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045660408/embalse-de-colomera-circular-by-tozar-and-benalua-de-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas&lt;/a&gt;, which offers significant elevation gain and views of the reservoir.

Q: What historical sites or natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Colomera's routes are rich in history and natural beauty. You might encounter a significant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3682350" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Bridge&lt;/a&gt;, a Visigothic necropolis, or the ruins of a castle above the village. Natural features include the prominent Colomera Reservoir, natural caves like Cueva de Ollafría, and areas rich in ammonite fossils. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1055470632/gollizno-route-and-moclin-castle-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gollizno Route and Moclín Castle Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for example, combines varied terrain with historical points of interest like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5277382" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Moclín Castle&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any routes that pass by the Colomera Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The Colomera Reservoir is a central feature of the region, and many touring cycling routes run parallel to its waters, offering stunning views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045660408/embalse-de-colomera-circular-by-tozar-and-benalua-de-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, providing excellent vistas of the reservoir.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Colomera?
A: The touring cycling routes in Colomera are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars. Reviewers often praise the varied terrain, the scenic olive groves, and the beautiful views of the Colomera Reservoir, highlighting the rewarding experience of cycling in this diverse landscape.

Q: Are there any family-friendly touring cycling routes in Colomera?
A: While many routes in Colomera are classified as difficult, there are 8 moderate routes that might be more suitable for families or those looking for a less strenuous ride. These routes still offer the beautiful scenery of the region without the most challenging climbs.

Q: How long do the touring cycling routes typically take to complete?
A: The duration of routes varies depending on their length and difficulty. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1045660408/embalse-de-colomera-circular-by-tozar-and-benalua-de-las-villas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Embalse de Colomera — circular by Tózar and Benalúa de las Villas&lt;/a&gt;, a 33.7 km difficult route, typically takes around 2 hours 32 minutes. Shorter, moderate routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Roman Dam of Barcinas loop from Embalse de Cubillas&lt;/a&gt; (32 km) can be completed in about 1 hour 53 minutes.

Q: What is the best time of year to go touring cycling in Colomera?
A: Colomera offers diverse cycling conditions throughout the year. Given its altitude of 862 meters, spring and autumn generally provide pleasant temperatures for cycling. Summers can be warm, while winters might offer cooler, crisp rides. Always check local weather forecasts before heading out.

Q: Are there any routes that feature the Cubillas Reservoir?
A: Yes, some routes extend to include the Cubillas Reservoir, offering different scenic views. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40381150" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir – Roman Dam of Barcinas loop from Embalse de Cubillas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular moderate route that leads through olive groves and past the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1627469" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cubillas Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Where can I find viewpoints along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes in Colomera offer panoramic views, especially from elevated points. The varied terrain with ascents and descents often leads to stunning vistas over the landscape, including the Colomera Reservoir. Look for routes that traverse higher ground or mention significant elevation gain for the best viewpoints.
tips: Church from the early 16th century, built on the site of an old mosque. The temple combines different construction styles in the transition between Gothic-Mudejar and Renaissance. Its square tower stands out above the rest of the construction.

Located on the Colomera y de las Juntas riverbed, the Colomera reservoir was built between 1982 and 1989. Since its inception, the reservoir has been used to produce electricity, supply water to the towns in the area and the irrigation of the fields.
